reason_for_decision: The article clears the pass threshold by tying automation levels to authority, consequence, and operator trust instead of treating automation as a headcount question.
strengths: The article is strong on authority boundaries and why consequence level should shape automation depth.
It explains clearly how weak human-in-the-loop design can still fail if evidence quality is poor.
The recommended architecture of automating lower layers while preserving operator authority for consequential actions is sound.
summary_assessment: This is a strong pass. The article is disciplined, risk-aware, and practically useful for teams defining automation and operator oversight in surveillance systems.
